growth_records.strain: MK-D1 (Lokiarchaea, Asgard archaeon; Imachi et al. 2020 isolate)
growth_records.medium: anaerobic co-culture (not stated in this review); isolated from deep marine sediment, Kumano region, Japan
growth_records.cite: Schleper et al. Asgard archaea: have we found our microbial ancestors?. The EMBO Journal (2026). DOI:10.1038/s44318-026-00719-x. [PMC12993045]
growth_records.snippet: P. syntrophicum required a long and labor-intensive cultivation effort, mostly because of its slow growth...includes long lag phases and a doubling time of 14-25 days.
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (14-25 days) explicitly reported in review text (citing Imachi et al 2020/2024 primary isolation studies). Converted days to hours for doubling_time_h field (14 d=336 h, 25 d=600 h) by unit conversion only, no rate math. mu = ln2/doubling_time would give ~0.0012-0.0021 h^-1 if calculated, but not computed into growth_rate_per_h since only a secondary-source range is available and no single reproducible value is stated; left growth_rate_per_h null to avoid inventing a point estimate from a range. No medium composition, temperature, or pH given in this review article; organism is cultivated only as a syntrophic co-culture, not axenic.
